question:
Will Yuki Tsunoda be the 2025 Drivers Champion?

Description: This market will resolve according to the driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2025 F1 season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

As soon as the results for the final scheduled race of the 2025 F1 season are known this market will resolve.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2025 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), this market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

As soon as the results for the final scheduled race of the 2025 F1 season are known this market will resolve.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2025 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), this market will resolve to “No”.

evidences.content: close global Welcome to GPFans CHOOSE YOUR COUNTRY NL GB GPFans GB GPFans Are you a F1 Fan? Follow GP Fans Edition NL GB IT ES-MX US Yuki Tsunoda, British GP, 2025, generic # Yuki Tsunoda admits Red Bull NOT happy with his performance Yuki Tsunoda, British GP, 2025, generic — Photo: © IMAGO Change your timezone: ## Yuki Tsunoda admits Red Bull NOT happy with his performance Tsunoda has struggled since his promotion to Red Bull Kerry Violet Google Red Bull star Yuki Tsunoda has revealed that his performances with the F1 team so far this year have not done enough to impress. The Japanese racer was promoted to Red Bull's main F1 team after just two races in 2025, when Christian Horner and Helmut Marko decided Liam Lawson was not up to the role of Max Verstappen's team-mate. [...] ## Tsunoda on Marko and his F1 expectations Just one week on from that race in Silverstone and Red Bull has undergone a major change. Team principal Christian Horner was sacked from his role with immediate effect, and although the team's future beyond this season remains rocky, it isn't likely to change Tsunoda's chances of finishing the year alongside Verstappen. evidences.title: Yuki Tsunoda | Former F1 driver | Statistics | RacingNews365
evidences.content: Yuki Tsunoda had a good season, scoring no less than seventeen points for the Italian team. This resulted in the Japanese driver finishing fourteenth in the drivers' championship. This contributed to the exciting battle for seventh place in the constructors' championship, which was ultimately won by Williams. In 2024, the team changed its name to Visa Cash App RB. The season began with Daniel Ricciardo as his teammate, but after the Hungarian Grand Prix, he was replaced by Liam Lawson for the remainder of the season. The Japanese driver ultimately scored seventeen points (not thirty, this is a repetition of the previous number), and there briefly seemed to be an exciting competition between him and Lawson for a Red Bull seat alongside Verstappen, but that opportunity ultimately passed him [...] ## Teams and teammates | Yuki Tsunoda Yuki Tsunoda | Team | Teammate | Season | | AlphaTauri | Pierre Gasly | 2021 | | AlphaTauri | Pierre Gasly | 2022 | | AlphaTauri | Nyck de Vries/ Liam Lawson/ Daniel Ricciardo | 2023 | | Visa Cash App RB | Daniel Ricciardo / Liam Lawson | 2024 | | Visa Cash App RB | Isack Hadjar | 2025 | | Red Bull Racing | Max Verstappen | 2025 | © Red Bull Content P...

evidences.content: Issue 3 ARTICLE 1: GENERAL PRINCIPLES 1.1 Formula One World Championship 1.1.1 The FIA will organise the FIA Formula One World Championship (the "Championship") which is the property of the FIA and comprises two titles of World Champion, one for drivers and one for constructors. It consists of the Formula One Grand Prix races which are included in the Formula One calendar and in respect of which the ASNs and organisers have signed organisation agreements with the FIA. All the participating parties (FIA, ASNs, organisers, competitors and circuits) undertake to apply as well as observe the rules governing the Championship and must hold FIA Super Licences which are issued to drivers, competitors, officials, organisers and circuits. 1.1.2 The Championship and each of its Competitions are [...] 1.1.2 The Championship and each of its Competitions are governed by the FIA in accordance with the Regulations. 1.1.3 In the sense of the Regulations, terms referring to natural persons are applicable to any gender. 1.2 Regulatory Framework 1.2.1 The Regulations applicable to the Championship are the International Sporting Code (the “Code”), the Formula One Technical Regulations (the “Technica...

evidences.title: TECH WEEKLY: The Spa tweaks that show Red Bull aren’t giving up on 2025 success
evidences.content: 088-25%20RED%20BULL%20SPA.jpg Red Bull continue to develop their 2025 RB21, which arrived at Spa with an extensive aerodynamic update. This incorporated the front wing, front suspension fairings, sidepod and engine cover, and complements the new floor introduced two races earlier in Austria. The front wing features revised geometry for the first and second flap elements, to adjust the distribution of pressure across those two elements in a way which has been found in simulation to increase the load on the wing as a whole. Trackside engineering chief Paul Monaghan commented, “It’s been an ongoing development process. We saw gains in CFD [computational fluid dynamics simulations] and we judged we had the time and money available to make it.” [...] TABLE%20%20RED%20BULL%20SPA%20%20%20ARRjpg.jpgCLOCKWISE FROM TOP LEFT: Revised radiator inlet has changed split line between sidepod and top bodywork. The low-downforce rear wing used for the Sprint race and the bigger Suzuka-style wing used for the Grand Prix. Verstappen used the new front wing throughout but ran with a bigger top flap for the race, so as to balance the car around the bigger rear wing. New fairings around the front susp...

evidences.title: F1 season predictions: Our picks for 2025’s champions, surprises and top moments - The Athletic
evidences.content: PI: Antonelli. I mean, I did just say he’d grab two podiums, didn’t I? I am keeping an eye on Gabriel Bortoleto. He won the F3 and F2 titles in back-to-back years (just like Russell, Leclerc and Piastri). That counts for something. MB: For ‘best,’ I want to read ‘most fun.’ Isack Hadjar is going to be emotional, lively, and he might just outstrip a Racing Bulls teammate in Yuki Tsunoda who could feel a little lost following his winter Red Bull snub. There were just two-tenths between the pair in terms of their quickest testing laps in Bahrain and I’m looking forward to seeing how Hadjar fares from here. What you should read next F1’s next generation arrives: Meet the five rookies set to transform the 2025 season [...] MC: Hamilton. Yes, the seven-time world champion. The final few years of his time at Mercedes drastically contrasted the championship seasons and Hamilton failed to match Russell head-to-head, sometimes due to setup experiments and other times just not clicking with the car. Mercedes simply lacked consistency in the car’s performance, which left questions about what the 40-year-old is capable of. PI: Gasly. At 29, the Frenchman is now the unquestioned top driver at...

evidences.title: Yuki Tsunoda remains at RB for 2025 season - ESPN
evidences.content: ## ESPN # F1 # Yuki Tsunoda remains at RB for 2025 season Copy Link MONTREAL, Canada -- Yuki Tsunoda will stay at RB in 2025 after Red Bull's junior team confirmed it had taken up an option to retain the Japanese driver for another season. Tsunoda has made a strong start to the 2024 season, scoring 19 points in the first eight races, and appeared to be of interest to a number of rival teams for 2025. The news that he will remain at RB heaps extra pressure on his teammate Daniel Ricciardo, who does not have a deal confirmed for next year and could come under threat from RB's reserve driver Liam Lawson. [...] "The step up he has made this year is simply phenomenal, and he keeps surprising us all, race after race. There's no doubting his natural speed to which he has now added a much more mature approach and this combination makes him a very quick and consistent performer, and a great team player. "We continue to get a better understanding of what he needs from us and vice versa, so we are progressing together, Yuki as a driver and the team, as Visa Cash App RB. We share the same ambitions, so there are several good reasons to continue our journey together. He still has a lot to gi...
